factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across all segments.

evidence.text: Our second quarter 2023 results reflect increased demand for our services, as revenue increased in all of our segments as compared to the second quarter of 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand across all segments, supported by evidence of increased revenue.

factors.summary: Backlog and remaining performance obligations are growing.

evidence.text: Our remaining performance obligations and backlog were $12.48 billion and $27.20 billion as of June 30, 2023, representing increases of 41.8%, and 12.9% relative to December 31, 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Both backlog and remaining performance obligations have increased significantly, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin projects.

evidence.text: The mix of revenues based on the types of services we provide in a given period will impact margins, as certain industries and services provide higher-margin opportunities. Our larger or more complex projects typically include, among others, transmission projects with higher voltage capacities; pipeline projects with larger-diameter throughput capacities; large-scale renewable generation projects; and projects with increased engineering, design or construction complexities, more difficult terrain or geographical requirements, or longer distance requirements.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The shift towards higher-margin projects suggests improved profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating.

evidence.text: Our second quarter 2023 results reflect increased demand for our services, as revenue increased in all of our segments as compared to the second quarter of 2022.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating organic growth across all segments is a strong positive indicator.

factors.summary: Revenue growth is driven by real demand.

evidence.text: Our second quarter 2023 results reflect increased demand for our services, as revenue increased in all of our segments as compared to the second quarter of 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth driven by real demand indicates a positive trend.

factors.summary: Renewable energy and underground infrastructure segments are growing fastest.

evidence.text: With respect to our Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions (Renewable Energy) segment, the transition to a reduced-carbon economy is continuing to drive demand for renewable generation and related infrastructure (e.g., high-voltage electric transmission and substation infrastructure), as well as interconnection services necessary to connect and transmit renewable-generated electricity to existing electric power delivery systems. With respect to our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions (Underground and Infrastructure) segment, in 2022 and through the second quarter of 2023 we experienced strong demand for our services focused on utility spending, in particular our gas distribution services to natural gas utilities that are implementing modernization programs, and our downstream industrial services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Rapid growth in key segments like renewable energy and infrastructure is a positive development.

factors.summary: Supply chain challenges are improving.

evidence.text: Despite these positive longer-term trends, certain of our customers experienced supply chain challenges during 2022 and into the first half of 2023 that resulted in delays and shortages of, and increased costs for, materials necessary for certain projects, particularly sourcing restrictions related to solar panels necessary for the utility scale solar industry. Based on indications within this market during the second quarter of 2023, we expect the supply chain challenges and sourcing restrictions related to solar panels to improve throughout the remainder of the year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving supply chain challenges are expected, which is positive for cost management.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable but contribute to margin pressure.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses. The increase was partially attributable to an aggregate $28.7 million increase in the following items to support business growth: compensation expense, largely associated with increased salaries due primarily to headcount growth; and travel and related expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2022

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased labor costs contribute to margin pressure, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving.

evidence.text: Operating income was positively impacted by a $13.0 million decrease in corporate and non-allocated costs, which includes amortization expense, as well as a $11.9 million increase in operating income for our Electric Power segment, a $28.8 million increase in operating income for our Renewable Energy segment and a $17.3 million increase in operating income for our Underground and Infrastructure segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2022

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows significant improvements in operating income across multiple segments and a decrease in corporate costs, indicating clear positive trends.

factors.summary: Supply chain is improving.

evidence.text: Based on indications within this market during the second quarter of 2023, we expect the supply chain challenges and sourcing restrictions related to solar panels to improve throughout the remainder of the year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company expects supply chain challenges to improve, which is a positive development for operations.

factors.summary: Positive forward guidance is expected.

evidence.text: We expect the strong demand for our services will continue. Our remaining performance obligations and backlog were $12.48 billion and $27.20 billion as of June 30, 2023, representing increases of 41.8%, and 12.9% relative to December 31, 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence points to strong demand and increasing backlog, indicating positive future expectations.

factors.summary: Management is confident with strong backlog and demand.

evidence.text: We expect the strong demand for our services will continue. Our remaining performance obligations and backlog were $12.48 billion and $27.20 billion as of June 30, 2023, representing increases of 41.8%, and 12.9% relative to December 31, 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is supported by strong backlog and demand growth, reflecting a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain challenges.

evidence.text: Despite these positive longer-term trends, certain of our customers experienced supply chain challenges during 2022 and into the first half of 2023 that resulted in delays and shortages of, and increased costs for, materials necessary for certain projects, particularly sourcing restrictions related to solar panels necessary for the utility scale solar industry.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include ongoing supply chain challenges and increased costs, which are negative factors.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on growth in renewable and infrastructure solutions.

evidence.text: The transition to a reduced-carbon economy is continuing to drive demand for renewable generation and related infrastructure (e.g., high-voltage electric transmission and substation infrastructure), as well as interconnection services necessary to connect and transmit renewable-generated electricity to existing electric power delivery systems.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The focus on renewable and infrastructure solutions aligns with positive long-term demand trends.

factors.summary: Supply chain and energy costs are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: These conditions and events can negatively impact our financial results due to, among other things, the termination, deferral or delay of projects, reduced productivity and exposure to significant liabilities due to failure of electrical power or other infrastructure on which we have performed services. However, severe weather events can also increase our emergency restoration services, which typically yield higher margins due in part to higher equipment utilization and absorption of fixed costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While there are risks, there are also potential benefits, leading to a mixed signal that doesn't clearly indicate a positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Expected supply chain improvements are a near-term catalyst.

evidence.text: Based on indications within this market during the second quarter of 2023, we expect the supply chain challenges and sourcing restrictions related to solar panels to improve throughout the remainder of the year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected supply chain improvements are a strong positive catalyst for the near term.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing.

evidence.text: During the six months ended June 30, 2023, increased revenues resulted in $165.8 million of net cash provided by operating activities. Available commitments under our senior credit facility and cash and cash equivalents as of June 30, 2023 were $1.77 billion.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures indicate investment and growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable.

evidence.text: Available commitments under our senior credit facility and cash and cash equivalents as of June 30, 2023 were $1.77 billion.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable debt leverage suggests no significant change in financial structure.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: During the six months ended June 30, 2023, increased revenues resulted in $165.8 million of net cash provided by operating activities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in free cash flow shows enhanced cash generation capabilities.

factors.summary: MA activity is accretive with the acquisition of Blattner.

evidence.text: Our acquisition of Blattner Holding Company and its operating subsidiaries (collectively, Blattner) in the fourth quarter of 2021, has had a significant incremental impact on our ability to perform these services.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Accretive M&A activity with Blattner indicates significant positive development.

factors.summary: Construction infrastructure spending is growing.

evidence.text: Our second quarter 2023 results reflect increased demand for our services, as revenue increased in all of our segments as compared to the second quarter of 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in construction infrastructure spending reflects increased demand and opportunities.
